response.justification: Bogdan brings 16+ years of solid general software engineering experience with Staff-level tenure and strong TypeScript depth, and his recent work at re:cinq on MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure is genuinely relevant. However, the role demands 8+ years of production AI solutions experience, and Bogdan's explicit AI work only began in April 2025. Python — the primary language for the GenAI stack — does not appear in his skills. There is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. The gap between what the role requires and what the resume demonstrates is too wide to justify an interview at Staff level.
strengths: Hands-on experience building MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers and agentic workflow infrastructure at re:cinq, directly matching a listed key technology
Extensive Staff-level engineering history with demonstrated ability to mentor teams, run workshops, and deliver talks — relevant to the customer-facing technical-leadership dimension of the role
Proven full-stack breadth across TypeScript/Node.js, cloud infrastructure (AWS CDK), distributed systems, and DevOps, providing a strong foundation for GenAI integration work
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills list despite being a primary required language for the GenAI stack
No evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or named multi-agent frameworks — the core technical surface of the role
AI-specific experience is limited to roughly two months at re:cinq; the role requires 8+ years of production AI solutions, a gap that cannot be bridged by general engineering tenure
